Welcome to Gold Snipers

Welcome to the official manual for the Gold Snipers EA and **Copier Bridge**. This document details the trade management engines, features, and bridge capabilities of the system, organised from top-to-bottom to mirror the on-chart dashboard layout and desktop bridge interface.

---

1. Initial Setup, Upgrades & Connection Guide

Before activating the EA on your charts, you must complete the one-time authorization link between your Telegram account and the trading terminal.

🚀 The First-Time Activation Wizard

When you run the Gold Snipers Bridge (GoldSnipersBridge.exe) for the very first time, it initiates an interactive, high-speed setup console:

  • License Key: A dialog will appear asking for your Gold Snipers license key (format: GS-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX). This key is provided after purchase and is required before anything else.
  • Secure Authentication: Enter your phone number (with country code, e.g., +1...). The console will ask for the OTP code sent to your Telegram App, followed by your 2-Step Verification Password (if enabled). The Telegram API credentials are pre-embedded — no manual entry required.
  • Channel Subscriptions: The Bridge will print a list of your active groups/channels. Type the numbers corresponding to the channels you want to monitor (e.g., 2, 5, 9).

🔑 License Key System

Gold Snipers uses a cloud-based license key system. Here's how it works:

  • Obtaining a License: Once you purchase a license from your provider, your license key (format: GS-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX) is automatically generated and displayed on the checkout success screen, allowing you to copy it and download the installer immediately.
  • Entering Your Key: On first launch, the Bridge displays a dialog asking for your license key. Enter it once — it's stored securely on your machine.
  • Changing Your Key: Click Change License Key (green button, top-right of API Settings) to enter a new key at any time.
  • Machine Binding: Your license key is bound to your computer. It cannot be copied to another machine without admin assistance.

📦 License Tiers & Feature Matrix

The software is distributed in three distinct product tiers, designed to fit different trading volumes and system requirements:

  • MT5 Instances: The number of MetaTrader 5 terminals you can run simultaneously with the Bridge. You can configure multiple instances in the Bridge's API Settings panel (click Add MT5 Instance).
  • MT5 Accounts: Each license tier limits the number of unique MT5 broker accounts the EA can register. For example, Starter allows 1 MT5 account, Pro allows 3, and Expert allows 6.
  • Manual Trading: The EA's manual trading panel works on any MT5 terminal where the EA is attached, as long as a valid license key is loaded. No separate manual trading license is needed.
  • Tier Enforcement: All tier limits are enforced at multiple levels — UI controls are greyed out for restricted features, input parameters are clamped on initialization, and admin commands (Telegram SET) are blocked at the engine level. Switching tiers takes effect on the next bridge restart.
FeatureStarter TierPro TierExpert Tier
MT5 Instances1 terminalUp to 3 terminalsUp to 6 terminals
Unique Broker Accounts1 accountUp to 3 accountsUp to 6 accounts
Trading Symbol LockGold/XAUUSD onlyAll SymbolsAll Symbols
Max Grid LevelsCapped at 3 levelsCapped at 5 levelsUp to 10 levels
TP TargetsTP1–TP3 onlyTP1–TP5 onlyTP1–TP10
Stealth TP/SL ModeDisabled (Forced to ON)Allowed (Stealth/ON)Allowed (Stealth/ON)
Low-Latency Socket IPCDisabled (File-based)Enabled (TCP Sockets)Enabled (TCP Sockets)
AI InsightsDisabledDisabledEnabled
Analytics DashboardDisabledEnabledEnabled
Telegram Bot ControlDisabledEnabledEnabled

⚡ Seamless Upgrades (Zero Re-Configuration)

When installing a new version or upgrading the software, you do not need to re-enter your credentials or repeat the setup wizard:

  • Active Session Protection: All authorizations, API keys, and channel choices are saved securely in your local data directory (specifically gsae_session_go.json and bridge_config.yaml).
  • Automatic Upgrade Recognition: The new Bridge binary automatically detects these existing files on startup. It immediately bypasses the setup wizard and logs you straight into active monitoring within 2 seconds!

🔧 MT5 Expert Advisor Setup

Before the EA can trade, you must configure the following settings in MetaTrader 5:

  • Open MetaTrader 5 → Tools → Options → Expert Advisors
  • Check Allow algorithmic trading
  • Uncheck "Disable algorithmic trading when the profile has been changed"
  • Uncheck "Disable algorithmic trading when the account has been changed"
  • Check Allow DLL imports
  • Check Allow WebRequest for listed URL and add: https://hxlqbctnkqqtueuyqyyv.supabase.co
  • Click OK, then re-attach the EA to your chart

2. Dashboard Risk Management & Setup Metrics

The top-right quadrant of your Copier Dashboard features a dynamic, real-time Risk Projection Matrix. This engine displays your active sizing parameters and instantly calculates your maximum capital exposure before you fire your ladder.

  • ANCHOR COUNT: The number of initial market orders executed immediately at the current market price. (e.g., an Anchor Count of 3 will fire 3 separate positions upon execution).
  • ANCHOR LOT: The specific lot size assigned individually to each market (Anchor) order.
  • PENDING COUNT: The total number of pending limit orders placed down the ladder grid. Each level is a single pending order spaced at your configured Ladder Step interval. A Pending Count of 5 will generate 5 distinct pending levels below your entry (for BUY) or above your entry (for SELL).
  • PENDING LOT: The lot size assigned individually to each pending limit order.
  • STOP LOSS (PIPS): The protective distance (in pips) for the entire grid family, measured relative to the primary anchor entry price.
  • MAX RISK CALCULATOR: A dynamic, real-time math engine projecting your worst-case total loss scenario:
  • The Assessment: The EA instantly calculates the exact dollar and percentage loss if your initial Anchors are hit, AND every single configured Pending Layer is 100% filled, before the entire family hits your Stop Loss.
  • Dynamic Colour Warning: Displays in Green (SAFE) for conservative exposure (under 2% of balance), scaling into Amber (CAUTION) at 2–5%, and Red (DANGER) at 5%+, ensuring you maintain ultimate oversight of your account margin.
  • R:R Ratio: Displays the Reward-to-Risk ratio for the configured TP/SL setup (e.g., R:R 1:3.2 $230.40). Shows TP/SL OFF when TP mode is disabled. The dollar figure represents the maximum potential reward at the deepest TP target.

Risk Display Format

The risk row on the Copier panel reads:

```

RISK: SAFE (0.08%) $72.00 | R:R 1:3.2 $230.40

```

FieldMeaning
SAFE / CAUTION / DANGERColour-coded risk status based on % of balance at risk
(0.08%)Total risk as percentage of account balance
$72.00Maximum dollar loss if all entries + all pending layers are filled and SL is hit
R:R 1:3.2Reward-to-Risk ratio (deepest TP reward vs worst-case SL loss)
$230.40Maximum dollar reward at the deepest configured TP target

3. Dual-Channel Independent Copier Panels

Provides two completely independent trading profiles (CH1 and CH2) without any settings leakage into your manual setup:

  • Dual Sub-Tabs: Click CH1 or CH2 at the top of the panel to instantly switch between and configure distinct preset blocks (lot sizes, grid counts, and TP targets).
  • Isolated Trade Execution: When you click BUY or SELL on the Copier panel, the EA takes a snapshot of that channel's settings (lot size, TP targets, SL, etc.) and uses only that snapshot for the trade. Your Manual Dashboard settings are never touched or overwritten.
  • Channel Identity: Features dedicated fields for CHANNEL NAME and CHANNEL ID directly under the selection tabs, keeping your signal routing perfectly organised and visible.
  • Connection Status Footer: The bottom of the Copier panel displays the current IPC connection mode:
  • ~50ms (Green status) — Active TCP socket connection to the bridge. Signals are delivered instantly via socket IPC.
  • ~250ms (Orange status) — Socket IPC is disabled or unavailable. The EA falls back to reading the signal .txt file from disk. Higher latency but no configuration required.

4. Take Profit Targeting & Paging

Configure up to 10 customised Take Profit levels without cluttering your chart:

  • TP1 - TP5 / TP6 - TP10 Toggle: Click the TAKE PROFIT TARGETS TP1-TP5 header bar at the top of the panel to switch between TP1–TP5 (standard targets) and TP6–TP10 (deep runner targets). Click again to switch back.
  • Columns Design: Displays targets in clean, tactile vertical columns, styled for effortless scanning and real-time editing of pips and partial shave percentages (%).

How Partial Close Percentages Work & The Runner

Each TP level has a close percentage — the proportion of the position's original opening volume that is closed when that target is hit.

The key rule: Percentages are cumulative and applied to the original lot size, not the remaining volume.

For example, if your original position is 1.00 lot and you have:

LevelClose %Cumulative %Volume ClosedRemaining
TP140%40%0.40 lot0.60 lot
TP230%70%0.30 lot0.30 lot
TP320%90%0.20 lot0.10 lot

The final remaining portion (in this example, 10%) is called the runner — the last slice of the position that is not assigned to any TP level.

To leave a runner: Simply ensure the total assigned percentages across all levels sum to less than 100%. Any unassigned portion keeps running with its TP target still active.

To close everything at TP3: Set TP1 + TP2 + TP3 = 100% (the runner becomes 0%).

Tier Limits on Active Levels

TierActive TP LevelsLevels Beyond Cap
StarterTP1–TP3 onlyTP4–TP10 are inactive — set to 0% automatically
ProTP1–TP5 onlyTP6–TP10 are inactive — set to 0% automatically
ExpertTP1–TP10 (all active)None

Starter example (runner on TP3):

LevelClose %Notes
TP150%Closes 0.50 lot
TP230%Closes 0.30 lot
TP315%Closes 0.15 lot — runner is 5%
TP4–TP100%Not used — Starter only has 3 levels

The runner (5%) continues running with TP3 still as its target. Once TP3 is hit, the remaining 5% is the only position left and it was targeting TP3 so it also closes there.

Pro example (runner on TP5):

LevelClose %Notes
TP140%Closes 0.40 lot
TP225%Closes 0.25 lot
TP315%Closes 0.15 lot
TP410%Closes 0.10 lot
TP50%Runner — 10% keeps running to TP5
TP6–TP100%Not used — Pro only has 5 levels
💡 Note: Note: Any TP percentages set beyond your tier's limit (e.g. TP4 on Starter) are accepted by the EA while running but are zeroed on the next EA restart. They are also never created as ladder levels, so they never fire regardless.

4.5 Unified Take Profit & Stop Loss Stealth Mode

The TP/SL Mode button (on both the Manual and Copier panels) controls how the EA manages your Take Profit and Stop Loss levels. Click the button to cycle through three modes:

TP/SL Modes Explained

ModeButton TextWhat Happens
TP/SL OFFTP/SL OFFNo TP or SL levels are managed by the EA. Positions run with no targets.
TP/SL STEALTHTP/SL STEALTHBoth TP and SL are hidden from the broker. The EA monitors price locally and closes positions when targets are hit.
TP/SL ONTP/SL ONHard TP and SL levels are sent directly to the broker. The EA places visible targets on the broker's server.
💡 Note: Note: Starter tier licenses are forced to TP/SL ON and cannot access Stealth mode.

How Stealth Mode Works

When TP/SL STEALTH is active, the EA keeps your real SL and TP targets entirely in its own memory — invisible to your broker. This prevents stop-hunting and spread manipulation.

  • Virtual Targets: The true, narrow SL and TP targets are stored locally in MT5 Global Variables (GSAE_VSL_ and GSAE_VTP_), not on the broker's server.
  • Broker Sees Nothing: Orders are placed with TP = 0 and (optionally) a wide Emergency SL as a safety net.
  • Real-Time Monitoring: The EA's StealthMonitor engine checks every 200ms. When price touches your virtual TP or SL, the EA instantly closes or partially closes the position.
  • All Features Work: Trailing stop, break-even, harvest, and manual SL/TP adjustments all update the virtual targets locally — nothing is sent to the broker.

Emergency SL Safety Net

When Stealth Mode is active, you can optionally place an Emergency Stop Loss on the broker as a safety net. This protects your account if the EA loses connection (terminal crash, internet outage, power failure).

SettingDefaultEffect
Use Emergency SL (InpUseEmergencySL)false (OFF)When ON, the broker sees a wide SL beyond your virtual SL as a safety net. When OFF, the broker sees SL = 0 — fully hidden.
Emergency SL Multiplier (InpEmergencySLMultiplier)2.0How much wider the Emergency SL is compared to your real SL. A multiplier of 2.0 means the Emergency SL is placed at 2x your normal SL distance.

How the Emergency SL works:

  • Your real SL is at, say, 2450 (50 pips below entry at 2500).
  • With multiplier 2.0, the Emergency SL is placed at 2400 (100 pips below entry).
  • If the EA crashes, the broker will close the trade at 2400 — worse than your intended 2450, but prevents unlimited loss.
  • If the EA is running, it monitors the virtual SL at 2450 and closes before the Emergency SL is ever reached.

When to use Emergency SL:

  • ON — Recommended for unattended trading (VPS, overnight). Provides a safety net against EA/terminal failures.
  • OFF — Maximum stealth. The broker sees SL = 0, TP = 0. No visibility into your targets. Best when you're actively monitoring or accept the disconnect risk.

Chart Visualization

When Stealth Mode is active, dashed lines can appear on your chart:

  • VSL (Red Dashed): Your virtual Stop Loss target. Enable via Show Visual SL Targets on Chart input.
  • VTP (Green Dashed): Your virtual Take Profit target. Enable via Show Visual TP Targets on Chart input.
  • Emergency SL (Orange Dotted): The broker-side Emergency SL safety net (only visible when Emergency SL is enabled).

Analytics & Exit Tracking

All stealth exits are logged in your local SQLite Analytics database with clear exit comments (STEALTH_TP or STEALTH_SL), while broker-side history comments remain completely generic — no trace of stealth mode visible to your broker.

5. Core Entry Modes: Grid vs. Single

The GRID ON / SINGLE ON toggle button controls how your trade entries are structured:

  • SINGLE Mode:
  • Opens a single set of market orders at the current price level.
  • No grid limit orders are created.
  • GRID Mode:
  • Opens your anchor trades immediately at the current market price.
  • Simultaneously places pending limit orders below your entry (if Buying) or above your entry (if Selling) at regular price step intervals.

6. Harvesting & Adaptive Profit Defense

Controlled via the HARVEST ON / OFF toggle button, Harvesting is an intelligent, automated risk-defence engine designed specifically for active Grid entries.

  • Objective: Secures profits on upper trade tiers during deep market pullbacks, allowing your portfolio to recover capital incrementally.
  • How it Works: When the price dives deep enough to trigger discount limit orders, the engine steps in to protect your higher-priced entry tiers. It automatically moves the Stop Loss of previous upper tiers to their own entry prices (+1.0 pip cushion).
  • The Advantage: On any minor market bounce, your upper "underwater" tiers are instantly liquidated at 100% Break-Even, freeing up your margin while leaving your deepest discount tier active to run all the way to its full profit target!
  • Threshold Guard: Uses a mathematical safety threshold (at least 50% of your grid step distance) to ensure that minor spread noise or slippage never triggers a harvest prematurely.

How Harvest Interacts with Break-Even

When Harvest is ON, the EA forces Break-Even reference mode to ENTRY regardless of your BE MODE setting. This ensures that each tier's SL moves to its own entry price (not the shared anchor), which is critical for the harvest logic to work correctly.

Trigger Sequence:

  • The EA waits for price to reach the configured Trigger BE level (e.g., TP1 or TP2).
  • Once triggered, the Break-Even engine moves the SL of all qualifying trades to their entry price + 1.0 pip (configurable via InpHarvestPips).
  • Upper tiers that are underwater are now locked at break-even — they close on the smallest bounce.
  • The deepest discount tier remains active with its original TP target, running for maximum profit.

Configuration

SettingWhereDescription
HARVESTCopier/Manual toggle buttonON enables harvest engine, OFF disables it
TRIGGER BECopier/Manual panel (TP1–TP5)The TP level at which BE kicks in for harvested tiers
BE MODECopier/Manual panelAutomatically forced to ENTRY when harvest is ON
InpHarvestPipsEA Input (Advanced)Safety cushion added to entry price (default: 1.0 pip)

7. TP AUTO Mode

The TP AUTO mode automatically distributes Take Profit targets across your entries, extracting quick early profits from market entries while letting deeper limit entries run for maximum gains.

How it Works (Example Setup)

  • Multi-Anchor Count: 3 Market Entries (Anchors)
  • Grid Count: 10 Pending Limit Levels
  • Deepest Configured Level: TP10

Phase 1: Strategic TP Assignment

When you fire your grid in TP AUTO mode, the EA assigns Take Profit targets based on your Anchor Strategy setting:

Distributed TP Mode (Default):

  • Anchors (Market Orders): Your primary market entries are distributed sequentially across your nearest configured Take Profit targets to ensure rapid capital recovery:
  • Anchor #1: Targets TP1 (Immediate profit & risk mitigation trigger)
  • Anchor #2: Targets TP2
  • Anchor #3: Targets TP3
  • Pending Tiers (Limit Grid Orders): ALL limit orders placed down the grid (Tiers 1 through 10) are automatically assigned to target the single deepest configured Take Profit (e.g., TP10). This ensures that any deep pullback fills run all the way back up for maximum profit extraction.

Unified TP Mode:

  • All Anchors Target Deepest TP: Every market entry targets the single deepest active Take Profit and participates in cumulative partial percentage shaving.
  • Pending Tiers (Limit Grid Orders): Same as Distributed — all limits target the deepest configured TP.

Phase 2: Execution & Risk Mitigation

When the market triggers your limits and moves back in your favour:

  • Anchor Target is Hit:
  • As soon as your primary Anchor hits its assigned target (e.g., TP1), it liquidates for an immediate profit.
  • This triggers the Auto Break-Even engine, which automatically moves the Stop Loss for all remaining active trades and pending limits to your entry price plus a safety cushion (+1.0 pip).
  • Your entire remaining grid exposure is now 100% risk-free.
  • Deep Entries Run to Finish:
  • Any limit trades that were triggered during the pullback are now completely secured at Break-Even and will run to target the maximum Take Profit (e.g., TP10), capturing massive gains on major trend expansions.

8. Anchor Strategy Toggles (Unified vs. Distributed)

Control exactly how profits are extracted across multiple market entries:

  • DISTRIBUTED TP: (Standard View) Distributes your Take Profit targets sequentially across your open market positions (e.g., Position 1 targets TP1, Position 2 targets TP2). In this mode, Anchors bypass the partial shave engine and exit 100% at their staggered targets. Ideal for quick scale-outs.
  • UNIFIED TP: (Auto Shave Integration) Directs ALL active market entries to target the single deepest active Take Profit currently configured (scaling fully up to TP10). Crucially, this mode authorizes the core FamilyShaveEngine to process all anchors, executing partial percentage shaves sequentially at each target rather than bypassing shave triggers. Perfect for extracting maximum capitalization while locked into progressive profit-securing!

🧬 High-IQ Institutional Shave Architecture

To prevent the standard industry trap of "shrinking residuals" (where EAs calculate percentages on shrinking remainders, leaving microscopic open trades indefinitely), the Gold Scalper EA implements an institutional-grade, self-correcting scaling algorithm:

  • Initial Entry Caching: The moment your ticket opens, the EA mathematically "memorizes" the Original Opening Lot Volume (e.g., 0.50 Lots) inside its persistent cache memory.
  • Cumulative Target Grid: At every TP level, the engine computes the ideal Cumulative Remaining Volume rather than isolated closed amounts. (For 0.50 lots @ 20% increments: TP1 target is 0.40 lots, TP2 target is 0.30 lots).
  • Self-Correcting Cuts: The EA calculates the exact gap between the *active live volume* and the *ideal target volume* at the next trigger. This mathematically compensates for any broker slippage, spread drift, or manual micro-adjustments, ensuring a guaranteed perfect liquidation at your final target!

9. Zone Execution Mode (Signal Zone vs EA Settings)

When a signal provider sends a price zone (e.g., BUY @ 4500-4503), the EA can handle it in two ways:

  • EA Settings (Default): The EA uses its own Pending Count and Ladder Step settings. The zone top becomes a single entry price, and pending orders are placed using your configured spacing. This gives you full control over grid density regardless of the signal format.
  • Signal Zone: The zone geometry overrides your Pending Count. The EA calculates the number of pending orders based on zone width divided by your configured Ladder Step, then places them using your channel's Ladder Step spacing. For example, a zone of 4500-4560 with a 30-pip Ladder Step generates 2 pending orders spaced 30 pips apart.

Configure this per channel in the Bridge's Parsing Settings tab: Respect Zone toggle (default: OFF = EA Settings).

10. Precision Break-Even Engines (BE Settings)

The EA features automated and customisable Break-Even protection, allowing you to surgically manage position lifecycles and lock in profits directly from the panel:

  • BE MODE: (e.g., BE MODE: ANCHOR / ENTRY)
  • ENTRY: (Individual Mode) Each individual position moves its Stop Loss to its own unique opening fill price, securing every entry independently.
  • ANCHOR: (Massive Lock-In Mode) Forces EVERY trade in the active grid (including your deeper discount limit fills) to move its Stop Loss to align with the high original Anchor Entry Price. This means that any deeper discount entries are locked into massive, locked-in gains on a trend continuation! If the anchor entry price cannot be resolved (e.g., all anchor orders were closed or the family ID lookup fails), the BE engine automatically falls back to ENTRY mode for that family, using each trade's own entry price instead.
  • TRIGGER BE: (e.g., TRIGGER BE: TP2) Milestone selector to set exactly at which TP target the EA initiates Break-Even logic (e.g., lock in safety once price hits TP1 or TP2).
  • Secured Profit Buffer (BE Lock-In): Once triggered, the EA automatically moves your Stop Loss to your entry price plus a configurable safety cushion (defaulting to +1.0 pip) to cover transactional spreads.

10.1 CANCEL PENDING Button (Delete Pending Limits on TP)

The CANCEL PENDING button (e.g., CANCEL PENDING: TP2) allows you to toggle the auto-cancel feature for pending limit orders on the fly:

  • CANCEL PENDING: OFF: Keeps all pending limit orders active.
  • CANCEL PENDING: TP1 through TP5: Automatically deletes remaining pending limits as soon as the price hits your configured Take Profit milestone (e.g., set to CANCEL PENDING: TP2 to clear pending orders immediately once price triggers TP2).

11. Trailing Stop Engine

The EA features an automated Trailing Stop Engine that progressively locks in profit by moving the Stop Loss closer to the current price as the trade moves in your favour. It operates independently from Break-Even and only activates after Break-Even has already been secured (SL at or past entry) AND the trade has moved at least InpTrailActivation pips (default 100 pips) beyond break-even.

Trail Modes

Cycle through modes via the TRAIL button on the Copier or Manual panel (TRAIL: OFF → TRAIL: STEP → TRAIL: CANDLE → TRAIL: FRACTAL):

ModeBehaviourBest For
STEPSL follows price at a fixed distance behind it, moving in discrete increments. Uses InpTrailDistance (default 20 pips) and InpTrailStep (default 5 pips).Trending markets with steady momentum
CANDLESL is placed behind the previous closed M15 candle's Low (BUY) or High (SELL) + optional padding (InpTrailPadding).Structure-based traders who trust candle wicks
FRACTALSL is placed behind the lowest Low / highest High of the last 5 M15 bars + optional padding. Provides a wider, more conservative trail.Conservative trailing with swing structure protection

How Each Mode Works

STEP Mode (Default):

  • Calculates a theoretical SL at Current Price - InpTrailDistance (BUY) or Current Price + InpTrailDistance (SELL).
  • Only moves the SL if the new level is at least InpTrailStep pips above (BUY) or below (SELL) the current SL.
  • This prevents micro-adjustments on every tick while still locking in profit during strong moves.

CANDLE Mode:

  • Reads the previous closed M15 candle (index [0] = most recently closed bar).
  • For BUY: places SL at M15 Low - InpTrailPadding.
  • For SELL: places SL at M15 High + InpTrailPadding.
  • Only moves SL toward profit (never backward).

FRACTAL Mode:

  • Scans the last 5 M15 bars to find the structural swing point.
  • For BUY: places SL at Lowest Low of 5 bars - InpTrailPadding.
  • For SELL: places SL at Highest High of 5 bars + InpTrailPadding.
  • Provides the widest trail of all modes — ideal for catching major trend moves.

Key Rules

  • BE-First + Activation Gate: The trail engine is dormant until Break-Even is secured (SL already at or past entry) AND the trade has moved at least InpTrailActivation pips into profit. This prevents premature trailing on entries that haven't reached safety or sufficient profit distance yet.
  • One-Way Only: SL only moves toward profit. Once the trail sets a level, it never reverts.
  • Per-Channel: C1 and C2 have independent trail mode settings. Manual trades use the manual trail mode.
  • Stealth Compatible: In Stealth TP mode, the trail engine uses the virtual SL (not the broker SL) for comparisons.

Configuration

ParameterDefaultDescription
InpTrailDistance50.0 pipsHow far behind current price the SL sits (STEP mode)
InpTrailStep10.0 pipsMinimum increment the SL must improve before modification (STEP mode)
InpTrailActivation100.0 pipsMinimum profit (in pips past break-even) before trail engine activates
InpTrailPadding0.0 pipsExtra cushion behind candle/fractal structure (CANDLE/FRACTAL modes)
Trail ModeOFFCycle via TRAIL button: OFF → STEP → CANDLE → FRACTAL

Trail Engine Priority Chain

```

Position Opens

→ Break-Even dormant (SL untouched)

→ TP1 Trigger Hit → Break-Even moves SL to entry + 1 pip

→ Trade profits ≥ InpTrailActivation pips past break-even

→ Trail Engine activates → SL begins trailing behind price

→ Price reverses → Trail SL protects accumulated profit

```

12. Signal Entry Cutoff & Telegram Routing

Interacts dynamically with the Telegram Bridge to capture, verify, and route incoming community alerts:

  • TG CMDS: (e.g., TG CMDS: INACTIVE / ACTIVE) Master bypass valve per-channel. Set to ACTIVE (Green) to let remote signals trigger live trades. Set to INACTIVE (Grey) to safely ignore them.
  • Surgical Code-Level Gate: Toggling to INACTIVE permanently seals the core execution loops. The Expert Advisor will aggressively reject incoming remote files, log a "Skipping Signal" event in your terminal, and prevent any order dispatching, providing a total defensive shield.
  • ENTRY CUTOFF / SIG GUARD: (e.g., ENTRY CUTOFF: 30p / SIG GUARD: 30p) A pip-based chase protection that prevents entries too far from the signal's entry price. Cycles through values like OFF, 10p, 20p, 30p, 40p, 50p per channel.
  • The Block: When the current market price has moved away from the signal's entry price by more than the configured pip limit, the EA converts the anchor to a pending limit order at the signal's entry price instead of executing at market — preventing chasing.
  • The Chase: If the live market price has moved from the signal entry but sits inside your chosen cutoff distance, the EA dynamically approves the market fill, ensuring you don't miss critical entries. If price has moved beyond the cutoff, the anchor becomes a pending limit order at the signal's entry price instead.
  • How it works: If current price is beyond the entry + cutoff zone, the EA converts the market anchor to a limit order at the signal's entry price and logs SIG GUARD: ANCHOR→LIMIT, ensuring you never overpay for a late entry.
  • Configuration: Set via the Copier panel ENTRY CUTOFF toggle button (cycles through OFF, 10p, 20p, 30p, 40p, 50p). Per-channel — C1 and C2 have independent settings.
  • Smart Channel Routing: The background engine dynamically maps incoming Telegram IDs to your configured channel presets (CH1 or CH2) to auto-execute with intended sizing and targets.
  • Stale Signal Guard: Protects account margin by automatically discarding any incoming signals older than 60 seconds.

13. Live Analytics & Equity Protect Matrix

Located at the bottom of the configuration dashboard in Full View, this analytics suite monitors real-time transaction metrics and implements a highly flexible, dual-action capital safety valve.

  • EQUITY PROTECT ($): A powerful, absolute-dollar safety guardian governing the entire family of trades currently open on the chart.
  • Take Profit Kill-Switch (Positive Value): If you enter a positive number (e.g., 1000), the EA monitors your floating Profit. Once the combined profit hits or exceeds this value, it triggers a Master Clearance to immediately close all active and pending orders, securing your cash profit.
  • Equity Protection (Negative Value): If you enter a negative number (e.g., -500), the EA establishes a hard cash-loss floor. If the combined floating drawdown drops to or below this negative threshold, the EA triggers an instant Master Clearance to flatten all positions, surgically protecting your account equity from runaway losses.
  • Deactivated (0.00): Setting the field to 0.00 completely disables the Equity Protect safeguard.
  • Profit Telemetry: Displays your cumulative, real-time floating profit or loss (P&L) for all active grid entries on the current chart. Dynamically updates colour from Green (Profitable) to Red (Drawdown) for instant visual assessment.
  • Spread Tracker: Measures and displays the broker's real-time Ask/Bid spread directly on the dashboard, helping you gauge liquidity and entry conditions.
  • Live Status Log Footer: A rolling diagnostic line displaying the most recent action performed by the trade engine (e.g., execution confirmations like SWEEP SELL @ MKT, automated adjustments, partial shaves, or server rejection alerts).

Multi-Instance Analytics

When running multiple MT5 instances, the analytics dashboard supports per-instance switching:

  • Instance Selector: A dropdown at the top of the Analytics tab lets you select which MT5 instance's data to view.
  • Automatic Selection: On startup, the bridge automatically selects the first configured instance.
  • Switching: Selecting a different instance from the dropdown instantly swaps the active database and refreshes all stats and deal history.
  • Database Mapping: Each instance maps to its own SQLite database file:
  • Instance 1: gsae_analytics_v2.db
  • Instance 2: gsae_analytics_v2_2.db
  • Instance 3: gsae_analytics_v2_3.db
  • Note: Analytics data is shown per-instance only. There is no cross-instance aggregated view at this time.

14. Manual Panel & Technical Intel Deck

Sitting to the immediate right of the configuration dashboard, the Manual Panel provides standalone discretionary execution power paired with an institutional-grade technical intelligence engine that evaluates real-time order flow and multi-timeframe trends.

  • ORDER FLOW (HTF Bias Score): An advanced analysis engine that computes structural bias on a Higher Timeframe (HTF, e.g., H1) using a proprietary Trade Confidence Matrix:
  • Multi-Factor Evaluation: Weighs confluence factors including Fair Value Gaps (FVGs), Liquidity Sweeps, Market Displacement, and algorithmic Kill Zones.
  • Output: Displays the dominant bias (BUY BIAS / SELL BIAS) alongside the mathematical confidence score in points (e.g., 80pts), giving you instant insight into institutional momentum.
  • TF SCANNER (Timeframe Matrix): A sequential micro-trend scanner evaluating market structure simultaneously across M1, M5, and M15 intervals. It ensures your rapid manual entries are fully synchronised with the broader macro Order Flow.
  • BID / ASK Telemetry: Dedicated real-time price display providing instant visual tracking of broker Ask/Bid quote mechanics.
  • Standalone Execution Controls: Complete manual sizing controls (LOT SIZE and hard STOP LOSS price inputs) isolated from Copier settings, paired with high-visibility BUY and SELL execution triggers.
  • Precision Layer & Trade Management Console: High-IQ manual overrides providing surgical control over entries and protection thresholds:
  • Direct LIMIT Buttons (BUY LIMIT / SELL LIMIT): Generates customised pending limit orders exactly at the price level typed into the corresponding input field.
  • Entry Pip Offset (centre input field): A configurable pip value displayed between the SELL LIMIT and BUY LIMIT buttons (e.g., 40). The EA uses this value to calculate the limit price relative to the current market price. For example, with 40, a BUY LIMIT is placed 40 pips below current price and a SELL LIMIT is placed 40 pips above.
  • TP Synchronization (BUY TP / SELL TP): Instantly modifies the Take Profit of ALL open positions and pending orders on the chart to align perfectly with the typed target price, perfect for mass-synchronising to key resistance zones.
  • SL Synchronization (BUY SL / SELL SL): Instantly overwrites the Stop Loss of ALL open positions and pending orders on the chart to match the typed protective price level.
  • CANCEL LIMITS: A one-click button that deletes all pending limit orders on the current chart without closing any active positions. Available on both the Manual panel and the Copier panel. Useful when market conditions change and you want to clear the board.

15. Intraday Extension Panels (Trades, Levels & Signals)

Expands a secondary visual intelligence dock when clicking the TRADES utility button on the top bar. Contains three sub-tabs:

Sub-Tab A: 💼 TRADES (Portfolio Dock)

Provides clean, grouped oversight of your active exposure:

  • Family Grouping: Bundles individual grid orders into their parent families (e.g., manual trades vs. channel grids).
  • Total Profit Tracker: Monitors combined real-time floating P&L specifically for each family.
  • One-Click Liquidation: Click the dedicated CLOSE button next to any family to instantly close that specific grid without affecting others.

Sub-Tab B: 📊 LEVELS (Technical Assistant)

An on-chart analyst that auto-generates critical market boundaries and session data:

Pivot Points & Levels:

  • Previous Day Data: Displays the previous trading day's High, Low, and Close prices at the top (e.g., Prev D1: H:4085.55 L:4000.30 C:4015.94).
  • Pivot Point (PP): Calculates the central pivot from the previous day's H/L/C — serves as the primary support/resistance magnet.
  • Resistance Levels (R1–R3): Three calculated resistance zones above the pivot, displayed with their exact pip distance from the current Bid price.
  • Support Levels (S1–S3): Three calculated support zones below the pivot, displayed with their exact pip distance from the current Bid price.
  • Pip Distance Matrix: Each level shows the real-time pip distance from current price (e.g., Res 1: 4067.56 (-436p)), enabling instant risk assessment.

Session Highs & Lows:

  • ASIA H/L: High and Low prices reached during the Asian trading session (00:00–08:00 UTC).
  • LON H/L: High and Low prices reached during the London session (08:00–16:00 UTC).
  • NY H/L: High and Low prices reached during the New York session (13:00–21:00 UTC).
  • These levels act as intraday liquidity zones — price often reacts at session extremes.

Sub-Tab C: 📡 SIGNALS (Live Market Intelligence)

A real-time signal intelligence dashboard that analyses market conditions before you trade. Displays the following panels:

Live Market Signal Dashboard:

  • BID / ASK / Spread: Real-time broker quote telemetry and current spread in points.
  • Session Timer: Shows the current trading session (e.g., SESSION: NEW YORK K) and countdown to the next session.

HTF Trend Bias:

  • Direction: Displays the Higher Timeframe (H1) trend bias as BULLISH, BEARISH, or NEUTRAL, derived from the institutional EMA bias engine.

Scanner Bias:

  • BUY / SELL Bias: The multi-timeframe scanner (M1, M5, M15) evaluates micro-trend alignment and outputs a directional bias with a confidence score (e.g., BUY BIAS / WAITING...).

Current Market Signal:

  • Shows the real-time signal from the HTF scanner (e.g., SELL BIAS (75pts)), combining structural analysis with quantitative scoring.

Buy & Sell Confidence Scores:

  • BUY CONF: Confidence grade and point score for a hypothetical BUY entry (e.g., BUY CONF: C (40 pts)).
  • SELL CONF: Confidence grade and point score for a hypothetical SELL entry (e.g., SELL CONF: A (75 pts)).
  • Grades range from A (high confluence) to D (weak setup).

Signal Criteria Breakdown:

A checklist of individual confluence factors, each showing Y (met) or N (not met):

CriterionWhat It Checks
HTF Bias AlignDoes the trade direction match the H1 trend?
Active FVGIs there an active Fair Value Gap in the trade path?
Liquidity SweepHas price recently swept a key high/low?
DisplacementIs there strong directional momentum (large body candles)?
Inside KillzoneIs the current time within a trading session killzone?
In Order BlockIs the price inside an institutional Order Block?
VWAP Buy OKIs price below VWAP (favourable for buys)?
VWAP Sell OKIs price above VWAP (favourable for sells)?

ATR Volatility Meter:

  • Displays the 14-period ATR value with a visual bar indicator and volatility classification:
  • Low (Quiet): ATR below average — potential breakout setup.
  • Moderate (Stable): Normal market conditions — standard trading environment.
  • High (Volatile): Elevated volatility — wider stops recommended.

Recent System Logs:

  • Rolling diagnostic log showing the most recent EA actions and bridge events (e.g., socket connections, SET command confirmations, signal processing results).

16. Ergonomic Action Buttons & UI Modes

The dashboard provides high-visibility utility controls on the top bar for rapid execution:

  • Execution Grids: Depending on view, rapid-fire controls provide instant trading power:
  • SELL & BUY: Instant order execution.
  • CLOSE LIMITS: Deletes all outstanding pending grid orders on the broker server.
  • CLOSE ALL: Flattens all open positions for this specific chart immediately.
  • Live Analytics (Full Mode): Populates the left panel with Equity Protection thresholds, live Floating Profit/Spread displays, and an integrated System Status log footer.
  • Operational States: Uses high-contrast operational colour states (Amber Gold, Oceans Cyan) to make active trading modes instantly recognisable.

17. Core Pip Scaling (Gold/XAUUSD Scaling)

The EA's pip calculation is optimised for Gold (XAUUSD):

  • 1 Pip on Gold (XAUUSD) = $0.10
  • 10 Pips on Gold (XAUUSD) = $1.00
  • A 50 pip Stop Loss translates exactly to $5.00 of price movement.
  • A 10 pip Ladder Step spaces out your limit tiers by $1.00 of price movement.

18. UI Modes Sync & Safety Engines

To prevent strategic conflicts, the EA automatically adjusts the panel buttons when selecting modes:

  • Enabling TP AUTO: Automatically configures Partials to GRID, Harvesting to OFF. This ensures the Distributed TP Grid runs safely and initiates Break-Even right when TP1 is hit.
  • Hardened Chart Layout Redraw: To maintain precise alignment when changing charts, altering timeframes, or loading MT5 profiles, the CHARTEVENT_CHART_CHANGE engine automatically cleanses visual elements, resets the UI initialization states, and forces a dynamic, pixel-perfect rebuild.
  • Intelligent Input Protection: Built-in guards ensure that typing new numbers into input fields is smooth, instant, and never loses focus during volatile markets.

19. The Copier Bridge Desktop Engine

The Telegram Copier Bridge (gs-bridge.exe) is the high-speed external engine that connects your community alerts directly to your trading terminal. It logs into your Telegram account, listens to your chosen signal channels, and transforms unstructured chat text into precise trade data delivered to the EA in under 50ms via TCP socket IPC.

1. Setup & Initial Credentials

When launched for the first time, the bridge initiates a secure interactive setup wizard:

  • API Registration: The Telegram API ID and Hash are pre-embedded in the bridge — no manual entry required. You do not need to obtain your own API credentials.
  • Secure Authentication: Asks for your Telegram phone number, followed by your Telegram App OTP and 2-step Verification Password (if active). The authentication state is stored securely in your local gsae_session_go.json file.
  • Channel Subscription Matrix: Reads and displays up to 50 of your active groups/channels. You select target channels by typing their corresponding list numbers (e.g., 1, 4, 7).

2. Multi-Instance Setup (Multiple Brokers / Accounts)

The bridge supports running multiple MetaTrader 5 terminals simultaneously, each connected to a different broker account or trading environment.

How It Works

Each bridge instance manages its own set of MT5 profiles. You configure these in the Meta Trader tab:

  • Single Instance (Default): If you only run one MT5 terminal, no configuration is needed. The default signal file is gsae_signal.txt and the EA reads it automatically.
  • Multiple Instances: To connect multiple brokers or accounts, run a separate MetaTrader 5 terminal for each one. Each instance gets its own signal file:
  • Instance 1: gsae_signal.txt
  • Instance 2: gsae_signal2.txt
  • Instance 3: gsae_signal3.txt
  • (and so on, up to gsae_signal6.txt)

Configuration Steps

  • Open each MetaTrader 5 terminal and attach the Gold Snipers EA to your chart.
  • In the EA inputs, set the signal file to match the bridge instance (e.g., gsae_signal2.txt for Instance 2).
  • In the Bridge's Meta Trader tab, click + Add MT5 Instance to create a new profile.
  • Assign your Telegram channels to the C1/C2 slots for each instance.
  • Click Save Channels & Restart to apply changes.

Channel Limit

Each bridge instance supports a maximum of 2 Telegram channels (C1 and C2). To copy signals from more channels, run additional bridge instances with their own MT5 terminals.

License Tier Limits

License TierMax MT5 Instances
Starter1 instance
Pro3 instances
Expert6 instances

3. Settings Templates

The bridge includes a built-in Settings Templates system that lets you save, apply, and share complete EA configurations across channels with one click.

Built-In Templates

The bridge ships with several pre-configured templates:

  • Conservative: 0.01 lot, 3 pending levels, 80 pip SL — ideal for small accounts
  • Standard: 0.02 lot, 5 pending levels, 50 pip SL — balanced risk/reward
  • Aggressive: 0.05 lot, 5 pending levels, 40 pip SL — higher risk, higher reward
  • Scalping: 0.03 lot, 3 pending levels, 30 pip SL — quick in-and-out trades
  • Sniper (Expert): 0.1 lot, 8 pending levels, 50 pip SL — full-featured expert setup

Creating Custom Templates

  • Open the Templates tab in the bridge.
  • Click Get Settings to import the current channel configuration from the EA.
  • Edit any fields (lots, TP targets, strategy toggles, etc.).
  • Click Save Template to store it as a custom template.

Applying Templates

  • Select a template from the list (built-in or custom).
  • Select the target channel from the dropdown.
  • Click Apply — the bridge sends all configuration values to the EA via admin commands.
  • The EA applies the settings and the bridge refreshes to confirm.

Template Sections

Templates can be sent in sections or all at once:

  • Entries & Lots: Anchor/Pending lot sizes, SL, ladder step
  • Anchor TP: Take Profit distances and close percentages for anchor orders
  • Pending TP: Take Profit distances and close percentages for pending orders
  • Strategy: Harvest, Grid/Single mode, TP/SL mode (ON/Stealth), Anchor (Unified/Distributed), Trail
  • Triggers: Break-Even mode, Trigger level, Cancel Pending level, Signal Guard

Template Management

  • Duplicate: Create a copy of an existing template for quick customisation.
  • Export/Import: Export templates as JSON files to share between bridge installations.
  • Delete: Remove custom templates (built-in templates cannot be deleted).

2. Low-Latency Signal Delivery (Socket IPC)

In the bridge's API Settings panel, scroll to the "Low-Latency Signal Delivery" section:

  • Enable Socket IPC: When checked, the bridge establishes a high-speed local TCP socket server. The EA connects to this socket and receives signals instantly via network-byte-order IPC, bypassing disk I/O entirely.
  • Performance & Ports: Reduces signal latency from ~250ms (file polling) to under 10ms (socket connection). It runs on port 28731 by default.
  • Multi-Instance: Each MT5 profile gets its own local port automatically (e.g. instance 1 on 28731, instance 2 on 28732, etc.).
  • Backward Compatibility: If the bridge has Socket IPC disabled, the EA silently falls back to file polling at a default 250ms interval. No configuration changes required.
  • MT5 Requirement: The EA must have "Allow DLL imports" enabled in the Properties → Common tab to utilise Winsock library connections.

2. Intelligent Parsing & Conversational Commands

The bridge's text interpreter is designed to extract precise trading data from diverse, unstructured formats:

  • Structure Extractor: Identifies Order Direction (BUY / SELL), Entry Zones (@1950 - 1948), Stop Losses (SL), and scales up to TP10 (TP1 to TP10 or consecutive TP listings).
  • Conversational Command Gate: Monitors for real-time administrative commands typed by Traders and instantly maps them to EA global actions:
  • CMD,CLOSE_ALL: Triggered by phrases like CLOSE ALL, CLOSE NOW, I AM OUT, EXIT TRADE, or CANCEL SETUP.
  • CMD,RISK_FREE: Triggered by phrases like RISK FREE, SECURE RUNNERS, MOVE TO BE, BREAK EVEN, or LOCK PROFITS.

3. Strict Anti-Noise Filter Suite

To protect your capital from unintentional activations, the bridge executes five rigorous filtering checks on every incoming message:

  • Strict Chatter Guard: Rejects any message containing zero numerical levels (no entries/TPs/SLs) unless it contains explicit entry confirmation tokens like NOW, MARKET, GO, or LIVE.
  • Media Isolation: Automatically ignores any message containing a Photo or Voice Note to avoid false parsing of charts or banter.
  • Forwarding Protection: Detects and automatically discards forwarded messages to eliminate cross-channel echo.
  • Status Updates Filter: Ignores messages stating "AT TPx" (e.g., "Gold at TP1") to prevent re-firing filled setups.
  • Keyword Matrix: Filters out any unstructured chatter that doesn't contain the explicit instrument identifiers GOLD or XAU (excluding emergency exit commands).

4. Multi-Message Signal Correlation (TP/SL in Second Message)

Some signal providers send their entry in one message and TP/SL details in a separate follow-up message. Enable this feature to prevent duplicate trades:

  • Enable: In the Bridge's Parsing Settings tab, toggle "TP/SL in Second Message" ON for the target channel.
  • How It Works: When enabled, the first message opens the trade immediately. A BUY NOW / SELL NOW signal with no entry price is executed as a market order using the EA's default TP/SL. If a second message arrives from the same channel within the configured time window, it is automatically treated as an UPDATE signal and assigned the first message's ID, so the EA updates the existing position's TP/SL instead of opening a duplicate trade.
  • Zone Wait (Hold Bare Signals):
  • Enable: Toggle "Zone Wait (Hold Bare Signals)" ON (this option is active when "TP/SL in Second Message" is checked).
  • How It Works: Instead of executing a market order immediately on the first bare message (e.g. BUY NOW with entry=0), the bridge holds the signal. If the second message with the entry zone, SL, or TPs arrives within the match window, the bridge merges the held signal's direction with the new message's pricing data. It then delivers a single combined trade signal with the entry price to the EA, allowing the EA to respect its Sig Guard settings (placing pending limit/stop orders instead of forcing a market execution).
  • Fallback / Discard: If the configured match window expires and no second message arrives with zone or pricing data, the held signal is discarded/rejected and a warning is logged (it is NOT traded blindly at market).
  • Match Window: Configurable time window (default 120 seconds, range 10-300). Only messages within this window are correlated.
  • TP/SL Parsing Dependency: The UPDATE signal is only processed by the EA if "Enable TP Parsing" or "Enable SL Parsing" is ON. If both are OFF, the EA ignores the UPDATE and uses its own default TP/SL settings.
  • Default: OFF — the bridge processes each message independently (original behavior).

5. Trading Schedule

Control when the bridge is active based on days of the week and time windows:

  • Enable: In the Bridge's Settings tab, check "Trading Schedule".
  • How It Works: When enabled, the bridge only runs during your configured hours. Outside the schedule, the bridge stops entirely — no Telegram connection, no signal processing. When trading hours resume, the bridge automatically restarts.
  • Per-Day Config: Each day (Monday–Sunday) has its own ON/OFF toggle and start/end time. Configure different hours for different days (e.g. different session times on weekdays vs weekends).
  • Overnight Schedules: If the end time is earlier than the start time (e.g. Friday 06:00 to Saturday 05:00), the schedule crosses midnight automatically — no need to configure the next day separately.
  • Default: OFF — the bridge runs 24/7 as before.
  • Note: Times use the system timezone of the machine running the bridge.

4. Advanced Smart Mechanics

  • Smart Split-Signal Merging: If an analyst posts an immediate entry signal (e.g., "BUY GOLD NOW") and then sends a follow-up message with the targets ("SL: 1930, TP1: 1950") within the match window, the bridge recognises the correlation. It automatically merges the updates to the original message ID, allowing the EA to update the active ladder cleanly instead of firing duplicate trades.
  • Deduplication Guard: Utilises an internal memory cache to drop identical Telegram event payloads generated when members add emoji reactions or edits that do not change text content.
  • Connection Heartbeat Matrix: Writes a secure timestamp (gsae_signal_heartbeat.txt) every 30 seconds. The MT5 EA actively monitors this file to display a visual "Live Connection" state on your dashboard.

5. The Copier Config Menu

Upon restarting, the bridge displays the GOLD SNIPERS banner and holds a 3-second non-blocking countdown. Pressing any key opens the configuration utility:

  • [1] Re-select Telegram Channels (Keeps Login): Instantly re-scans your Telegram account to select different channels (preserving your active login session).
  • [2] Full Factory Reset (Wipes all credentials & config): Deletes all local configuration files, saved authorization credentials, and local license settings to allow a completely fresh deployment.
  • [3] Cancel & Start Bridge Now: Immediately bypasses the menu and activates active signal monitoring.

6. Signal Tester (Bridge)

The Signal Tester tab in the Bridge provides a complete sandbox environment to test how signals are parsed, routed, and executed — without placing any real trades.

Overview

The Signal Tester shows exactly what happens to a signal at every stage of the processing pipeline:

  • Bridge Parsing — exclusion keywords, media/forward checks, lexicon matching, regex extraction
  • EA Routing — which profile (C1/C2/CX_), channel slot, and socket port the signal maps to
  • EA Settings — reads the live EA configuration (lot sizes, pending count, TP levels, modes)
  • Trade Simulation — anchor price (floor/ceil), pending ladder entry prices, SL, and TP levels

How to Use

  • Select a Channel: Choose the channel profile to test against (or "Global Default" for unmatched signals).
  • Paste a Signal: Enter a raw signal message in the input area.
  • Click "Test Signal": The bridge parses the signal and displays the full execution trace.
  • Review the Trace: Each step shows a pass/fail indicator and detailed explanation.

Quick Templates

Pre-built signal templates for common scenarios:

  • BUY/SELL Signal — Full signal with entry, SL, and TP levels
  • BUY/SELL Entry Only — Signal with entry price but no TP/SL (for multi-message testing)
  • Zone BUY/SELL — Signal with a price zone (e.g., @ 2345/2340 AREA)
  • Reply TP/SL — TP/SL values only (for testing reply-based UPDATE signals)
  • Close All / Risk Free / TP Hit — Command signals
  • Chatter / Exclusion — Non-trade messages to test filtering

Testing Multi-Message Signals (TP/SL in Reply)

Some providers send the entry in one message and TP/SL in a separate reply. To test this:

  • Click "BUY Entry Only" to load a signal with no TP/SL.
  • Click "Test Signal" — this stores the BUY in the session.
  • Click "Reply TP/SL" to load TP/SL values.
  • Click "Test as Reply" — the parser detects isReply=true with no direction but has TPs/SL, returning UPDATE.

Testing Multi-Message Override (Second BUY becomes UPDATE)

When a provider sends two BUY/SELL signals in quick succession, the second one becomes an UPDATE:

  • Enable "Multi-Message Mode" in Parsing Rule Overrides.
  • Test a BUY signal — stored in session.
  • Test another BUY signal within the time window — overrides to UPDATE.

Parsing Rule Overrides

The collapsible Parsing Rule Overrides section lets you toggle individual parsing rules per-test:

  • Enable CLOSE ALL / RISK FREE / TP HIT — Toggle command recognition
  • Enable TP Parsing — Whether take profit values are extracted from the signal
  • Enable SL Parsing — Whether stop loss values are extracted from the signal
  • Respect Zone — Whether TP/SL from zone signals are preserved
  • Multi-Message Mode — Whether second BUY/SELL becomes UPDATE

Overrides apply only to the current test and do not modify saved configuration.

TP/SL Parsing vs Respect Zone

The Enable TP Parsing and Enable SL Parsing toggles give fine-grained control over which values the bridge extracts from the signal. Respect Zone is a separate mechanism that strips both TP and SL when a price zone is detected.

Order of operations: TP/SL parsing toggles run first, then Respect Zone runs second.

TP ParsingSL ParsingRespect ZoneZone signal result
ONONOFFBoth stripped (Respect Zone wins)
ONONONBoth preserved
OFFONONTPs stripped, SL preserved
ONOFFONTPs preserved, SL stripped
OFFOFFanyBoth stripped, UPDATE rejected

This means you can keep TPs but discard SL (or vice versa) regardless of zone, giving you more flexibility than Respect Zone alone.

EA-side behaviour: When the signal includes an explicit SL price, the EA uses it directly (overriding the channel's pip-based SL setting). When the signal has no SL, the EA falls back to its configured SL pips from the anchor. TPs from the signal are always converted to pip distances and override the channel's TP presets.

Metadata Flags

  • Is Reply — Simulates a Telegram reply message (allows TP/SL-only UPDATE signals, skips asset token gate)
  • Has Media — Simulates a message with photo/video (auto-applies Ignore Media rule)
  • Is Forward — Simulates a forwarded message (auto-applies Ignore Forwards rule)

EA Settings Display

When the EA is running, the trace shows the live channel configuration:

  • Lot sizes — Anchor and Pending lot sizes
  • Entry count — Number of anchors and pending levels
  • Ladder step — Pip distance between pending levels
  • SL/TP — Stop loss and take profit distances in pips
  • Modes — TP mode (ON/STEalth/OFF), Grid/Single, Harvest, Trail, Break-Even reference, Anchor (Unified/Distributed)

Reference: All Signal Test Variations

The table below covers every major scenario you can test in the Signal Tester, using Gold (XAUUSD) at $4000 for all examples.

#ScenarioReply?Multi-Msg?TP/SL ParsingRespect ZoneExample MessageExpected Result
1Single full signalOFFOFFONONXAUUSD BUY @ 4000 SL: 3990 TP1: 4010 TP2: 4020Market BUY at 4000 with SL/TPs from signal. Ladder pendings below entry.
2BUY NOW (immediate market)OFFOFFONOFF/ONXAUUSD BUY NOWMarket order at live price. EA uses its own channel default SL and TP levels. No follow-up expected.
3Multi-Message: TP/SL in second messageOFFON (120s)ONONMsg 1: XAUUSD BUY NOW
Msg 2: ENTRY: 4000-3998 SL: 3990 TP1: 4010 TP2: 4020		Msg 1 executes market. Msg 2 overrides to UPDATE and preserves Msg 1's ID — EA updates SL/TP on the existing position.
4Reply-based UPDATEONOFFONONMsg 1: XAUUSD BUY @ 4000 SL: 3990 TP1: 4010
Msg 2 (Reply to Msg 1): SL: 3985 TP2: 4025 TP3: 4035		Msg 1 opens trade. Msg 2 gets Msg 1's ID via reply_to_msg_id — EA modifies SL to 3985 and adds TP2/TP3. No Multi-Message needed.
5BUY LIMIT orderOFFOFFONONXAUUSD BUY LIMIT 3985 SL: 3975 TP1: 4010Parsed as BUY LIMIT — EA places a pending limit at 3985 instead of a market order.
6SELL STOP orderOFFOFFONONXAUUSD SELL STOP 4015 SL: 4025 TP1: 3990Parsed as SELL STOP — EA places a pending stop at 4015.
7Zone entry, Respect Zone ONOFFOFFONONXAUUSD BUY @ 4000/3995 SL: 3985 TP1: 4020 TP2: 4030Entry zone 3995–4000 preserved. SL/TP from signal used. EA averages or uses bottom for ladder.
8Zone entry, Respect Zone OFFOFFOFFONOFFXAUUSD BUY @ 4000/3995 SL: 3985 TP1: 4020 TP2: 4030SL and TP stripped (Respect Zone off). EA uses its own channel SL/TP defaults.
9TPs only, No SLOFFOFFONN/AXAUUSD BUY @ 4000 TP1: 4010 TP2: 4020SL not parsed — EA falls back to its channel SL pip setting. TP levels from signal applied.
10SL only, No TPsOFFOFFN/A (TP OFF)N/AXAUUSD BUY @ 4000 SL: 3985TPs not parsed (TP Parsing OFF) — EA uses its channel default TPs. SL from signal used.
11CLOSE ALL commandOFFOFFOFFOFFCLOSE ALL XAUUSDParsed as CMD,CLOSE_ALL — no trade entry. EA closes all open positions.
12RISK FREE commandOFFOFFOFFOFFMOVE TO BREAKEVENParsed as CMD,RISK_FREE — no trade entry. EA moves SL to entry on all positions.
13TP HIT notificationOFFOFFOFFOFFTP1 HITParsed as CMD,TP_HIT_1 — triggers cancel-pending or break-even based on channel settings.
14AT TP noise (rejected)OFFOFF——GOLD AT TP1REJECTED. Bridge detects AT TP pattern and drops it as a status notification.
15Chatter (rejected)OFFOFF——Nice setup team!REJECTED. No asset token (GOLD/XAU), no numbers, no action keywords.
16Forwarded message (rejected)OFF + Ignore Forwards ONOFF——Forwarded: XAUUSD BUY @ 4000 SL: 3990REJECTED. Bridge detects the forward and ignores it.
17Reply with TP/SL, no parentONOFFONONSL: 3990 TP1: 4020Parsed as UPDATE — no direction in text, but Reply + TP/SL implies update. If no position exists, EA logs and skips.
18Multi-Message window expiredOFFON (10s)ONONMsg 1: XAUUSD BUY NOW
Msg 2 (15s later): SL: 3990 TP1: 4020		Msg 1 executes. Msg 2 outside the 10s window — processed as a fresh signal, not an UPDATE.

Key notes:

  • Reply = Telegram's native reply_to_msg_id linking. The bridge uses the parent message's ID as msg_id, so the EA correlates the update to the original position.
  • Multi-Message = Two separate (unlinked) messages correlated by time window. The bridge preserves the first message's msg_id in the second. Requires the "TP/SL in Second Message" toggle ON.
  • TP/SL Parsing & Respect Zone can be toggled independently per channel in the Parsing Settings tab. Overrides in the Signal Tester apply only to the current test.
  • Commands (CLOSE ALL, RISK FREE, TP HIT) bypass all parsing toggles and execute regardless of signal age.

Live Trade Simulation

For market orders (BUY/SELL without a specific entry price), the trace reads the live bid/ask from the EA and calculates:

  • Anchor price — Floor/ceil rounded to the nearest half-pip (Gold-specific)
  • Pending entries — Each ladder level calculated from anchor ± (step × pips)
  • Stop loss — Anchor ± (SL pips × pip value)
  • Take profits — Anchor ± (TP pips × pip value) for each configured TP level

20. Advanced Guide: Trade Exclusions, Command Routing & Copier Workflow

This section provides an advanced, user-friendly breakdown of how the Copier Bridge and the Gold Scalper EA interact under the hood, and how to surgically customise trade exclusions, keywords, and automated remote commands.

🔄 The End-to-End Copier Workflow

To help you understand exactly how your signals are copied from Telegram to MetaTrader 5, here is the lifecycle of a copied trade:

  • Signal Broadcast: A signal provider posts a message in your subscribed Telegram Channel (e.g., "BUY XAUUSD @ 2030, SL: 2020, TP1: 2035, TP2: 2040").
  • Bridge Parsing: The Copier Bridge Desktop Engine instantly intercepts the message, processes it through the Strict Anti-Noise Filters, and extracts the entry price, Stop Loss, and Take Profits.
  • Secure Delivery: The Bridge delivers the parsed signal to the EA via TCP socket IPC (~50ms latency). As a fallback, it also writes a .txt signal file inside the secure shared MetaQuotes Common directory.
  • EA Detection & Context Routing: The MT5 EA immediately receives the signal, matches the Telegram Channel ID to your preset tab (CH1 or CH2), and extracts the trade context.
  • Filters & Cutoffs Evaluation: Before execution, the EA verifies:
  • Is the signal older than InpTelegramMaxSignalAgeSec? (Default 10 seconds; if yes, discarded).
  • Does it contain any configured Trade Exclusions? (If yes, ignored).
  • Is the current market price within the Entry Cutoff range? (If yes, executed).
  • Instant Execution: The EA fires the anchor trades and pending limit grid precisely matching your channel settings (CH1 or CH2 lot size, steps, and take profits).

🛡️ How SL & TP Are Applied

The EA always uses your channel settings for Stop Loss and Take Profit — not the signal provider's values. This keeps you protected if a provider sends incorrect or dangerously wide targets.

  • Stop Loss: Always uses your channel's SL pip setting (e.g. 60 pips). Signal SL prices are ignored. Your stop is always calculated relative to your anchor entry price.
  • Take Profit: Signal TPs are only used when they are in the correct direction (above entry for BUY, below entry for SELL). If a provider sends TPs in the wrong direction (e.g. below market for a BUY), the EA falls back to your channel's TP presets automatically.
  • Ladder Step & Pending Count: When a signal contains a price zone (e.g. @ 4500-4503), the EA uses your channel's Ladder Step to space pending orders. The zone only determines how many pending levels are placed.

🚫 How to Configure Trade Exclusions (Keyword Blocking)

You can configure Trade Exclusions to prevent specific setups from firing. This is highly useful if a channel provider posts a setup you want to ignore, or posts standard chat messages that look like trades.

  • Exclusion Keywords Box: Located in your Copier UI under the Parsing Settings tab.
  • How to Add Exclusions: Enter comma-separated keywords into the Exclusions text box (e.g. pending, test, limit, break, demo).
  • Surgical Filtering: If an incoming message contains any of your configured exclusion keywords, the Bridge instantly blocks the signal. The EA will not open any trades, and the Copier log footer will record: "Signal skipped due to exclusion keyword filter."

💻 Automated Remote Commands (Conversational Triggers)

The Copier Bridge does not just fire new setups; it listens for active updates from Traders to automatically protect your open positions:

  • Breakeven Commands (CMD,RISK_FREE):
  • Triggers: Channel updates containing terms like risk free, secure profit, move sl to be, break even, lock profits, sl to entry, or secure now.
  • Action: The Copier Bridge translates this into a high-speed command. The MT5 EA immediately moves the Stop Loss of all active trades in that channel's family to their entry price plus a safety spread buffer, instantly locking in safety.
  • Close All Commands (CMD,CLOSE_ALL):
  • Triggers: Channel updates containing terms like close all, close now, exit trade, cancel setup, close gold, i am out, or exit now.
  • Action: The Copier Bridge immediately signals a total flat command. The MT5 EA forcefully liquidates all open positions and deletes all pending limit grid levels for that specific channel's family, securing your cash profits immediately.

21. Remote Admin Control Panel (Telegram Saved Messages)

The Remote Admin Control Panel allows you to fully control the EA from your phone via Telegram Saved Messages, without needing to access your computer.

🚀 How to Access

  • Open Telegram on your phone
  • Navigate to Saved Messages (your own chat with yourself)
  • Type /admin and send
  • The Bridge will reply with a numbered text menu

🎮 Main Control Panel

```

🎮 Gold Snipers Control Panel

Reply with a number:

  • ⚙️ C1 Settings
  • ⚙️ C2 Settings
  • 🟢 BUY NOW
  • 🔴 SELL NOW
  • 📊 Status
  • ❌ Close All
  • 🛡 Risk Free
  • 🚫 Cancel Limits

```

Reply with a number (e.g., type 1 and send) to select an option.

⚙️ Channel Settings Menu

Reply 1 or 2 to access C1 or C2 settings:

```

⚙️ C1 Settings

Reply with a number:

  • 💰 Lot Size
  • 🎯 Anchors
  • 📊 Levels
  • ✂️ Cutoff
  • 🌾 Harvest
  • 🪜 Partials
  • 🛡 Auto SL
  • 📏 SL Pips
  • 🎯 TP Mode
  • 🚫 Cancel Lvl
  • ⚡ BE Trigger
  • 📍 BE Ref
  • ← Back

```

Then reply with a number to set the value. Example flow:

```

You: /admin

Bot: 🎮 Gold Snipers Control Panel (menu)

You: 1

Bot: ⚙️ C1 Settings (menu)

You: 1

Bot: 💰 C1 Lot Size (options: 1. 0.01, 2. 0.02, etc.)

You: 5

Bot: ✅ C1 Lot Size updated to 0.20

```

💰 Instant Trading Commands

CommandAction
3Execute BUY ladder using current copier settings
4Execute SELL ladder using current copier settings
6Close all open positions and pending orders
7Move all SL to break-even + harvest buffer
8Delete all pending limit orders

📊 Status Queries

Reply 5 to receive real-time account telemetry:

  • Balance — Current account balance
  • Equity — Current equity
  • Open PnL — Combined floating profit/loss
  • Active Trades — Number of open positions

🔒 Security

  • Owner-only: Commands are accepted only from your Telegram account (detected automatically via Saved Messages)
  • No configuration required: Works out of the box — no bot setup, no API keys, no group invites
  • Private by design: All commands and responses stay entirely within your own Saved Messages

⚡ How It Works

  • You type /admin in Saved Messages
  • Bridge sends a numbered menu
  • You reply with a number (e.g., 1, 5, 0)
  • Bridge writes a command file
  • EA's background timer polls and executes within 250ms
  • EA writes a response JSON
  • Bridge sends confirmation back to Saved Messages

📝 Command Reference

The numbered menus translate into these internal commands:

```

SET C1 LOT_ANC 0.05 → Update Channel 1 anchor lot

SET C2 CUTOFF 15 → Update Channel 2 signal cutoff to 15 pips

CMD BUY_NOW → Execute buy ladder

CMD CLOSE_ALL → Flatten all positions

GET STATUS → Query balance, equity, PnL

```

22. Troubleshooting Guide

The bridge includes a built-in Troubleshooter tab (🔧 Troubleshoot in the sidebar under Help). This tool provides real-time system diagnostics and quick-fix actions without leaving the bridge.

Embedded Troubleshooter Tab

Open the Troubleshoot tab from the left sidebar to access:

FeatureDescription
System StatusLive indicators for socket port, bridge processes, EA processes (MT5 terminal), and pending signal files
Kill Stuck PortKills any zombie process holding port 28731 — useful when the bridge fails to start
Kill All ProcessesKills all other bridge and EA (MT5 terminal) processes; this instance stays running
Clear Signal FilesRemoves stale signal files from the MT5 Common/Files directory to prevent duplicate trades on restart
Full ResetCombines Kill All Processes and Clear Signal Files into a single operation

Reset Telegram Session

In Settings, the Reset Telegram Session button stops the bridge, deletes the session file, and prompts for fresh phone/code authentication on next start. Use this when changing your Telegram phone number without reinstalling.

Quick Status Checklist

CheckWhere to LookWhat You Want to See
Bridge is onlineBridge headerGreen status dot and connected phone number
Channel is monitoredBridge API SettingsTarget channel appears in the channel list
EA is attachedMT5 chart top-leftSmiling face icon next to EA name
AutoTrading enabledMT5 toolbarLarge "AutoTrading" button pressed/highlighted
EA connection modeEA Copier panel footerSOCKET MODE: Connected to bridge or FILE MODE: Polling signal file
License validBridge header / EA chartNo red license banner

Bridge Side — Signal Never Arrives

If the bridge Live Log does not show the incoming message, the message never reached the bridge:

  • Telegram session expired. Restart the bridge and re-authorise if you see auth errors.
  • Channel not selected. In the bridge API Settings, the channel must be ticked in the monitored list.
  • Ignore Media / Ignore Forwards is ON. If the provider sends the signal as a forwarded message or an image with caption, and the matching toggle is enabled, the bridge drops it.
  • Exclusion keywords. If the message contains any word in the channel's Exclusions list (e.g. ALERT, EDUCATION, SUMMARY), the bridge blocks it.
  • Trading schedule. If the bridge's Trading Schedule is enabled and the current time is outside the configured window, the signal is held or dropped depending on the build.

Bridge Side — Signal Arrives But Is Parsed Wrong

The bridge log shows the message, but the extracted direction, entry, SL, or TP is wrong:

  • Asset token missing. The parser needs to see GOLD, XAUUSD, or XAU in the message. Without one, the message is treated as chatter and rejected.
  • TP/SL Parsing toggles are OFF. In the channel's Parsing Settings, Enable TP Parsing and Enable SL Parsing must be ON for the bridge to extract TP/SL prices.
  • Respect Signal Zone. When ON, the bridge preserves the entry zone and passes SL/TP to the EA. When OFF, the bridge strips signal SL/TP and the EA uses its own channel defaults.
  • TP/SL in Second Message is OFF. If your provider sends XAUUSD BUY NOW first and the SL/TP in a second message, this toggle must be ON. With it OFF, the second message is not linked to the first trade.
  • Multi-message window too short. The default match window is 120 seconds. If the provider's second message arrives later than this, it is processed as a fresh signal, not an update.
  • Custom regex conflict. If you enabled a custom regex in the channel settings, make sure it does not accidentally override the standard parser.

EA Side — Signal Arrives But No Trade Opens

The bridge log shows a parsed signal and the EA receives it, but no position appears:

  • AutoTrading disabled in MT5. Open Tools → Options → Expert Advisors and check Allow algorithmic trading. Also press the large AutoTrading button in the MT5 toolbar.
  • EA not attached to the correct chart. The EA must be loaded on the same symbol as the signal, normally XAUUSD.
  • Allow DLL imports / WebRequest not set. In Tools → Options → Expert Advisors, enable Allow DLL imports and add https://hxlqbctnkqqtueuyqyyv.supabase.co to Allow WebRequest for listed URL.
  • License error. A red license banner or popup means the account is not authorised or the instance limit was reached. Click Reset MT5 License in the bridge if you changed brokers.
  • Changing Telegram phone number. Click Reset Telegram Session in the bridge Settings tab. This stops the bridge, deletes the session file, and prompts for fresh phone/code authentication on next start.
  • Duplicate EA detected. The EA refuses to run two instances on the same account + signal profile. Close the duplicate terminal.
  • Channel ID mismatch. The EA compares the signal's chat_id to the CHANNEL ID values configured in C1 and C2. If neither matches, the signal is logged as REJECTED: CHANNEL MISMATCH.
  • Channel is inactive. If the matching C1/C2 channel toggle is OFF, the EA logs CHANNEL X INACTIVE and skips the signal.
  • Signal too old. The EA rejects signals older than InpTelegramMaxSignalAgeSec (default 10 seconds). Common causes: PC clock drift, file-mode fallback latency, or the bridge being paused.
  • Entry Cutoff / Late Guard. For signals with a specific entry price, if price has moved farther than the channel's Entry Cutoff setting, the signal is rejected as LATE GUARD. This does not apply to BUY NOW / SELL NOW market orders (entry = 0).
  • Spread too high. If the current spread exceeds InpMaxSpreadPips, the EA blocks the entry.
  • Tier limits exceeded. Starter/Pro tiers enforce caps on grid levels, TP levels, and accounts. The EA clamps or blocks the trade if a limit is exceeded.

Multi-Message TP/SL Updates Not Working

For the two-message workflow (BUY NOW first, SL/TP second):

  • Enable TP/SL in Second Message in the channel's Parsing Settings.
  • Keep Enable TP Parsing and Enable SL Parsing ON.
  • Ensure the second message arrives within the configured window (default 120s).
  • The bridge preserves the first message's ID. If the second message is a Telegram reply to the first, it does not need the multi-message toggle — replies work via reply_to_msg_id automatically.
  • The EA must already have an active position for that message ID. If the position was closed or the signal was rejected, the update has nothing to modify.

Commands (CLOSE ALL, RISK FREE, TP HIT) Not Working

  • Commands are only recognised if the message matches the configured lexicons in Parsing Settings.
  • Management commands are never rejected for being stale, so an EA restart will still execute a recent command.
  • The EA de-duplicates commands by msg_id. Editing a command message and resending it will not trigger it twice.

Bridge-to-EA Connection Issues

  • Socket mode preferred. SOCKET MODE: Connected to bridge is the fastest path. If the socket is unavailable, the EA falls back to FILE MODE and reads the signal .txt file from disk every 250ms.
  • Firewall / antivirus. If socket mode never connects, check that local TCP traffic on the configured port is not blocked.
  • Wrong MT5 Common path. In file mode, the bridge writes to the MetaQuotes Common folder. If you run multiple MT5 installations, make sure the bridge's signal file path matches the terminal the EA is running in.

Where to Find Logs

SourceLocationWhat It Tells You
Bridge Live LogBridge Dashboard tabEvery incoming message, parse result, and write event
Bridge logs%LOCALAPPDATA%\GoldSnipersBridge\logs\Persistent log files for support tickets
MT5 Experts tabMT5 Toolbox → ExpertsEA decisions, rejections, and errors
MT5 Journal tabMT5 Toolbox → JournalTerminal-level errors (trade disabled, DLL denied, etc.)
Order cycle eventsEA Trades panel / Analytics DBPer-signal lifecycle: SIGNAL, REJECTED, OPEN, CLOSE

23. MT5 Input Settings Reference

Complete reference of all EA input parameters, organised by group as they appear in the MT5 Properties → Inputs tab.

Panel Scaling & DPI

ParameterDefaultDescription
InpUiScaleStart1.0Starting panel scale (0.75–2.0)
InpUiModeEnabledPanel resize mode — drag the bottom-right corner to resize
InpFontDpiAdjDynamicFont DPI compensation mode (Dynamic / Off / Manual)
InpFontDpiManual1Font reduction in points (only when Manual mode is selected)

Telegram Copier Defaults

ParameterDefaultDescription
InpSignalFilegsae_signal.txtSignal profile (1–6 for multi-instance)
InpC1_ID(empty)Channel 1 Telegram ID (auto-populated by bridge)
InpC1_NameChannel 1Channel 1 display name
InpC1_ActivetrueChannel 1 copier active (ON/OFF)
InpC1_LotAnc0.01Channel 1 anchor lot size
InpC1_LotPen0.01Channel 1 pending lot size
InpC1_Anchors1Channel 1 anchor trade count
InpC1_Levels5Channel 1 ladder levels (pending count)
InpC1_TPMode2 (ON)Channel 1 TP mode (0=OFF, 1=STEALTH, 2=ON)
InpC1_PartialstrueChannel 1 partials (ladder)
InpC1_HarvestfalseChannel 1 harvest
InpC1_AutoSLtrueChannel 1 auto SL move
InpC1_CancelPendingLevel2Channel 1 cancel pending level
InpC1_TriggerBELevel2Channel 1 trigger BE level
InpC1_AllowCmdsfalseChannel 1 allow Telegram commands
InpC1_LateGuardPips0Channel 1 signal cutoff (0=OFF, 5–30 pips)
InpC1_AncShavetrueChannel 1 anchor unified TP (false=Distributed)
InpC1_SL50.0Channel 1 stop loss (pips)
InpC1_TP1–TP1030/50/80/100/130/0...Channel 1 TP distances (pips)
InpC1_Pct1–Pct1020/20/20/20/100/0...Channel 1 TP close percentages
InpC1_TP_PEN1–PEN1040/70/110/150/250/0...Channel 1 pending TP distances (pips)
InpC1_Pct_PEN1–PEN1020/20/20/20/100/0...Channel 1 pending TP close percentages

*(Channel 2 parameters mirror Channel 1 with InpC2_* prefix.)*

Session Lines & Killzones

ParameterDefaultDescription
InpDrawSessionLinesfalseDraw session high/low lines on chart
InpRemoveSweptLevelstrueAuto-hide levels after price sweeps them
InpSessionLookbackDays2History days for high/low calculation
InpShowKillzonesfalseDraw killzone rectangles on chart
InpKillzoneLookbackDays2Trading days of zones to draw
InpZone1–4_StartHour/EndHourGMT+3 defaultsAsia, London, Overlap, NY session hours

Institutional Engine

ParameterDefaultDescription
InpTradingStyleSwingEMA bias strategy (Long-Term/Swing/Day)
InpHTFTimeframeH1Higher timeframe for bias calculation
InpDisplacementMult1.5Min body size vs avg for displacement
InpVolumeMult1.2Min volume vs avg for displacement

Basic Defaults

ParameterDefaultDescription
InpStartupModeCopier PanelStartup UI view mode (Copier/Full/Manual)
InpEnableTelegramtrueEnable Telegram signal bridge
InpLotSize0.01Default lot size per trade
InpStopLossPips50.0Default stop loss distance (pips)
InpUseDynamicATRfalseUse dynamic ATR-based SL/TP
InpATRPeriod14ATR indicator period
InpATR_SL_Mult1.5Stop loss ATR multiplier
InpATR_TP1_Mult1.5TP1 ATR multiplier

ParameterDefaultDescription

InpDrawOrderBlockstrueDraw order blocks on chart
InpDrawFVGtrueDraw FVG boxes on chart
InpDrawIOFArrowsfalseDraw IOF 50% mitigation arrows
InpEnableFVGMitigationAlertstrueShow IOF 50% FVG mitigation text

InpObStructFiltertrueRequire structure break for OB
InpObLinkFvgtrueOB must overlap its parent FVG

Safety & Execution

ParameterDefaultDescription
InpUsePipetrueUse TCP socket for low-latency signals
InpMaxSpreadPips6.0Max allowed spread (pips) to open trade
InpDefaultGuardPips10.0Max pip slippage for default guard mode
InpSafetyCapPips10Safety cap (pips) when signal cutoff is OFF

InpTelegramMaxSignalAgeSec10Max signal age (seconds) before rejection

Ladder & Harvesting
